meanings.definition: a shallow area in a stream that can be forded
a point where two lines (paths or arcs etc.) intersect
a junction where one street or road crosses another
a path (often marked) where something (as a street or railroad) can be crossed to get from one side to the other
(genetics) the act of mixing different species or varieties of animals or plants and thus to produce hybrids
a voyage across a body of water (usually across the Atlantic Ocean)

examples: The caravan covered almost 100 miles each day

meanings.definition: hinder or prevent (the efforts, plans, or desires) of
examples: What ultimately frustrated every challenger was Ruth's amazing September surge

meanings.definition: fold so as to resemble a cross
to cover or extend over an area or time period; ,
trace a line through or across
breed animals or plants using parents of different races and varieties
examples: cross a horse and a donkey
